Altar: Ancient hinges hide a spring — dust puffs as a needle darts from the stonework.
You disturb a ward carved shallowly into the base; something bites back.
Coffin: The lid shifts on a hair trigger; a cloud of dust and a jagged wire lash your forearm.
Rotten silk hides a spring — splinters drive deep as the sarcophagus bites shut on your hand.
Dais: A loose flagstone tilts; spikes meant for kneeling supplicants flash upward from the seam.
You brush carved steps; a counterweight groans and a weighted pendulum swings from the canopy.
Desk: A drawer runs on a false rail — a needle snaps from the back panel into your knuckles.
Papers hide a spring; a staple-gun trap fires staples into your palm before you can recoil.
Debris: Rusty wire loops tight around your wrist as you shift a beam.
Broken glass and a taut cord slice as weight shifts under your probe.
Hut: Thatch gives way to a snare line that yanks taut across your shins.
A peg in the wall is a trigger — a weighted club swings from the ridgepole.
Lectern: The reading rest pivots; a dart hisses from the lectern's hollow core.
You tilt the book rest; concealed springs spit splinters into your sleeves.
Obelisk: Runes flare cold; a needle slides from a carved groove as your palm meets stone.
Dust on the base hides a pressure plate — a crack opens and something stings your ankle.
Pedestal: The plinth rotates a finger's width; a wire noose snaps up from the seam.
You test the mount; a spring-loaded spike thrusts from the column's hidden seam.
Pile of Chests: A chest lid is wired to its neighbor — latches snap and needles crossfire between the stacks.
Your pull on one handle trips the pile; iron jaws clamp from a chest you did not touch.
Pile of Weapons and Armor: A halberd head slips its rest; the blade kisses your shoulder as the pile collapses inward.
Mail links hide a tripwire — rusted hooks bite as the heap shifts under your weight.
Pit: False boards snap; cold air rushes up as the pit mouth opens underfoot.
Your weight breaks a rotten cover; something sharp waits in the dark a span below.
Pool: You lean over still water; a glassed needle launches from the coping into your reflection.
Slippery stone hides a spring — your hand skids and a metal tooth scores your palm.
Quicksand: The crust looks solid; one step and wet sand claws at your boots and will not let go.
Dry surface shatters; the pit pulls at your calves faster than you can step back.
Rocks: A balanced stone tilts; crushed grit and a sharp edge rake your fingers.
You shift a slab; a concealed spring drives a splinter through your glove.
Rubble: Broken brick hides a spring — dust and a sting answer your reach into the heap.
A wire runs through the collapse; it snaps taut across your wrist as stones shift.
Rug: The weave hides a pressure plate; a needle thrusts from the floorboards beneath.
You tug an edge; a concealed latch flips and wire bites your wrist.
Sarcophagus: Carved reliefs are a switch; a dart hisses from the lid's lip as it lifts a hair.
Ancient seals break; something sharp in the joint rewards your curiosity.
Stack of Books: A false volume pivots; a needle darts from the spine gap of the neighbor tome.
Weight on the stack trips a cord — weighted folios slam shut on your fingers.
Stack of Crates: A crate shifts on a hair trigger; nails and splinters shear upward from the gap below.
You pry a lid; a second crate's latch snaps and fishhooks bite your forearm.
Statue: The base is hollow; your touch frees a counterweight and a blade scribes your shin.
Dust hides a spring in the plinth — pain answers before you see the mechanism move.
Tent: A guy line is a snare; it whips tight as you brush the canvas flap.
Peg and rope hide a spring — a needle flashes from the tent pole's collar.
Throne: The armrest is a lever; hidden springs spit splinters into your sleeve.
You test the cushion; a needle thrusts from the seat through fabric and padding.
Torture Rack: Rusty gears bite; a ratchet slips and the wheel spins, catching your sleeve and your skin.
Straps hide fishhooks; they bite as you trace the leather with your hand.
Treasure Pile: Coins shift on a plate; a needle thrusts from the heap's heart as you dig.
Glinting trash hides wire — it loops and cuts as weight slides under your reach.
Well: The coping stone tilts; your balance fails and iron teeth scrape your shin on the rim.
You lean on the windlass; a catch releases and the handle spins, cracking your knuckles.

default: A concealed needle flashes as you lean too close.
Dust flies — and something sharp answers your probing hand.
Your probe springs a hidden catch; pain follows faster than thought.
